text:
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDY REPORT

Patient ID: MW-2026-001
Specimen: Breast tissue, right upper outer quadrant
Biopsy Date: 2026-03-02

Clinical History: 48-year-old female with invasive ductal carcinoma of the breast.

Material Received: Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded tissue from breast biopsy on blocks B1, B2, and B3.

Immunophenotyping Results:

1. HER2/neu (Herceptest): Positive 3+ on block B1 (>30% of cells show strong membrane staining). Block B2 shows 2+ staining (10-30% weak to moderate). Block B3 shows negative staining (<10%).

2. Estrogen Receptor (ER): Positive, strong nuclear staining in approximately 85% of tumor cells on block B1. Moderate staining (70%) on block B2. Weak staining (40%) on block B3.

3. Progesterone Receptor (PR): Positive, strong nuclear staining in 90% of cells on block B1. Moderate staining on B2. Weak to negative on B3.

4. Ki67 (proliferation): Approximately 25% positive nuclei on B1, 35% on B2, and 20% on B3.

5. GATA3: Positive in tumor cells (90% of cells), confirming breast origin.

Interpretation: Hormone receptor positive (ER/PR+), HER2 positive breast carcinoma. Heterogeneous expression across blocks suggests possible tumor heterogeneity. Ki67 index ranges from low to intermediate.

Recommendation: HER2+ disease may be candidate for HER2-targeted therapy. ER+ status suggests benefit from endocrine therapy.

BREAST TISSUE IMMUNOSTAIN RESULTS

Specimen: Left breast excisional biopsy
Blocks: C1 (lateral), C2 (central), C3 (medial)
Date Received: 2026-02-28

HER2-neu (4B5 antibody) Interpretation:
- Block C1 (lateral): 2+ staining, approximately 20% of neoplastic cells with moderate membrane reactivity
- Block C2 (central): 1+ staining, weak and incomplete in <20% of cells
- Block C3 (medial): Negative, rare tumor cells show faint background staining

Estrogen Receptor (SP1 antibody):
- All blocks: Strong positive staining, 95% of tumor nuclei
- Pattern: Diffuse and uniform throughout specimen

Progesterone Receptor (PgR antibody):
- All blocks: Strongly positive, 98% nuclear staining
- Pattern: Slightly stronger in central zones (block C2)

ER/PR Status: Triple negative for HER2 (based on HER2 score <3), strongly hormone receptor positive.

Note: The heterogeneity in HER2 expression may warrant FISH (Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization) testing on block C1 for clarification given clinical significance.

Ki-67 Staining (MIB-1):
- Approximately 12% of tumor cell nuclei positive for Ki-67
- Indicates relatively low proliferative activity
- Consistent with low-grade carcinoma

Conclusion: HR+/HER2- ductal carcinoma with low proliferative rate.

SPECIAL STAINS AND IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY RESULTS

Patient: Age 60, Female
Specimen: Right breast needle core biopsy
Blocks: D1, D2, D3, D4 processed
Submission Date: 2026-02-25

Routine Immunostains Performed:

HER2 (Herceptest):
- Block D1: Moderate positive (2+), 20-30% of cells with membrane staining
- Block D2: Weak positive (1+), <10% of cells
- Block D3: Score 1+, minimal staining
- Block D4: Negative

Endocrine Receptors:
Estrogen Receptor (ER, clone SP1):
- Positive in 70-80% of tumor cells (blocks D1-D3)
- Strong intensity
- Block D4: 40% positive, weaker than other blocks

Progesterone Receptor (PR, clone SP2):
- Positive in 75% of cells on D1 and D2
- 50% positive on D3
- 20% positive on D4

Proliferative Index (Ki67/Mib-1):
- Block D1: 30% positive nuclei
- Block D2: 28% positive
- Block D3: 22% positive  
- Block D4: 15% positive

Note: This carcinoma shows hormone receptor expression with variable intensity. Ki67 index suggests intermediate proliferative rate. FISH testing recommended for HER2 given borderline IHC results.

Luminal A-like features with intermediate grade based on ER+/PR+ status and moderate Ki67.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL PANEL FOR BREAST CARCINOMA

Specimen: Excisional biopsy, breast tissue
Blocks Tested: E1 (lesion), E2 (adjacent tissue)
Date Processed: 2026-03-01

Immunophenotyp Results:

HER2 (4B5 Automated Stain):
- E1: 3+ staining with strong complete membrane reactivity in >30% of cells
- E2: Negative background skin/adipose tissue shows no HER2 expression

Estrogen Receptor (Ventana SP1):
- E1: Strongly positive, 99% of tumor cells with strong nuclear staining throughout
- E2: Benign tissue is ER negative (normal expected finding)

Progesterone Receptor (Ventana PgR):
- E1: Strongly positive, 95% of cells with strong nuclear signal
- E2: Benign tissue negative (expected)

HER2 Gene Amplification Status: HER2 3+ by IHC - reflex FISH testing initiated

Ki-67 Proliferation Index (MIB-1 clone):
- E1: 35% of tumor cells positive
- Mitotic rate: Elevated

Cytokeratin AE1/AE3: Positive in carcinoma, negative in surrounding tissue
Testing confirms epithelial origin of malignancy.

Clinical Interpretation: HER2 positive (3+), ER/PR+ breast carcinoma with elevated Ki67 and high mitotic rate. Consistent with Grade 2-3 invasive ductal carcinoma. Patient is candidate for HER2-targeted therapy combined with endocrine therapy.

HEMATOPATHOLOGY LABORATORY - IMMUNOSTAIN REPORT

Case: IP-2026-0847
Material: Breast core biopsy on slides and paraffin blocks
Blocks: F1, F2, F3 (3 levels from same specimen)
Analysis Date: 2026-02-26

Antibody Panels Tested:

Stromal Support Assessment:
Smooth Muscle Actin (SMA): Positive in myoepithelial layer in benign ducts (F1, F2, F3). Absent in invasive carcinoma zones.

Basement Membrane (Collagen IV): Intact around benign ducts, disrupted at invasive front.

Hormone and Growth Factor Receptors:
ER (Estrogen Receptor):
- F1: 85% positive cells, strong staining
- F2: 80% positive, strong
- F3: 75% positive, strong  
- Pattern: Nuclear with some cytoplasmic in occasional cells

PR (Progesterone Receptor):
- F1-F3: 78-88% positive cells, strong nuclear

HER2 by IHC:
- F1: Score 1+ (weak, incomplete in <10% cells)
- F2: Score 1+ (negative to weak)
- F3: Score 0 (negative)
- Verdict: HER2 negative by IHC

Proliferation Markers:
Ki67: 18-22% positive tumor nuclei across all 3 levels (intermediate proliferation)
Mitotic figures: 4-6 per 10 HPF

Final Assessment: Luminal A-type breast carcinoma (ER+/PR+/HER2-) with intermediate grade and moderate proliferative activity. Appropriate for hormone-based therapy without HER2-targeted treatment.

No additional biomarker testing indicated.
